What all of these remakes and reboots lack is heart. Too obsessed with big explosions and 'cool' looking scenes. Bigger is not better. The original film was so simple yet so fantastic. They had ONE T-Rex stalking them (and a few raptors). But the story telling, the relationships, and the suspense are what made it so good. Same with Fast and Furious. The first film is literally just a movie about car guys racing. Nothing complex. Now they go to space. It's ridiculous. Bring back simple, but good, story telling with these movies

Stop... please, just stop. You're no longer capable of producing a good dinosaur movie, Universal... and every attempt since 2013 makes it even more sad. You've lost touch with it completely. Dinosaurs are fascinating creatures, they enhance our phantasies. Even the idea of bringing those animals back to life in our modern world is mind blowing. That's what made the world fall in love with the first two movies back in the 90s. Then you started this "genetically enhanced dinosaur"- bs as if dinosaurs NEEDED TO BE GENETICALLY ENHANCED to be scary or interesting. And what is this "oh, there's a secret government project" sh** supposed to be. If I wanna see that, I'll watch The Expendables or something like that with the problem still being, that you no longer trust your own recipe... you don't have trust in yourself to produce an exciting dinosaur movie that works on its own.  The jokes were not funny, the characters were painfully stupid, the mutant dinosaurs were an insult to the real ones.  

To make this kind of movie tolerable to watch there must be extremely charismatic actors to pull it through. There must be a really funny sidekick or two. 

 Scarlett Johansson was incredibly dry, cold, unrelatable and boring. None of the actors had any charisma. The were no funny sidekicks ( the one that was apparently supposed to be one, wasn't funny because the jokes were lukewarm ). 

The characters lacked depth and their relationships didn't develop. They were all completely unrelatable. 

The first part of the movie was too long. They just talked and talked without saying anything interesting. 

When the action finally started, the scenes were almost like a parody to this series but not funny at all.

If there is another movie, it should be called "Cretaceous Park" - the plot? The world's richest man has created a new dinosaur theme park featuring dinosaurs with COMPLETE genetic sequences, no frog DNA, no gaps in the genetic sequence filled in. Thus the world sees, for the first time, what the dinosaurs REALLY looked like. His park will feature T-REX like he really was - covered in feathers, the raptors too. This would give the movie the same shock and awe as the first movie, Jurassic Park - because the audience is seeing dinosaurs as modern science believes they were, which is quite different to how we THOUGHT they looked back in the early 90s when Jurassic Park was released.

I thought it was only two islands. Did they just magically create another one to keep the franchise going?
Incorrect.

Well, according to the second Jurassic Park movie, there are, in addition to the original Isla Nublar, five islands called "Las Cinco Muertes" or "the five deaths". 

Isla Nublar was where the original park was built. It is quite a bit to the east of Cinco Muertes. It was where Jurassic Park as well as Jurassic World 1 and 2 took place. The island was eventually destroyed in a volcano eruption, so no more dinosaurs most likely.

On Cinco Muertes, Isla Sorna was where the dinosaurs where primarily bred (and where the second and third movie took place). That island is probably still inhabited by dinosaurs.

What is happening on Isla Matanceros, Isla Muerta, Isla Tacaño and Isla Pena, I don't know. Apprently they were all bought by InGen back in the day, and later that new evil corporation that I don't remember the name of. So perhaps this story happens on one of these.

You really should listen to childhood obsessed mega fans like me when we speak. We study this stuff more than you do.

After this film, make a prequel series made on JP1 than TLWJP on what went on with the parks up until the movies. Draw them out, putting love and dedication into each episode, and make the show last. Then, once that is done, let it settle. At this point, it's obvious you Hollywood people just are doing this for money. It's not your dream career to work in a dinosaur zoo. But try to be caring while the world unknowing waits for the real thing,  and provide society with what you are really for, entertainment. Do this, with Steven and David at the helm and them hand picking brilliant and tallented minds that get storytelling, and make people love this soon to be option for humanity again. The movies have not been realistic and quotable since TLW. That's bad. If you want people to learn from whatever you think is so bad about these animals, then give people the chills  with beautiful language. If you listen to me while your franchise is still the only option, then you'll get more people to listen. Thanks.

🦖 Jurassic Formula – Character Checklist

1. The Rugged White Guy – Usually a scientist or adventurer. Knows everything about dinosaurs. Becomes the hero.
2. The Pretty Woman – Smart, resourceful, but also there to balance the cast visually. May or may not fall for the hero.
3. The Black Guy – Often tech-savvy, funny, or sacrifices himself bravely. Rarely the lead.
4. The Asian Scientist – Mysterious, morally ambiguous, usually behind the genetic chaos.
5. The Kid(s) – Always present. In danger. Screams a lot. Somehow smarter than the adults sometimes.
6. The Greedy Businessman – Wants to monetize the dinosaurs. Definitely dies.
7. The Dinosaur Whisperer – (Newer films) Someone who magically bonds with raptors. Because… Hollywood.

I always perceived the main points of jurassic park, at least the novel, and original film in ways, as a metaphor for human arrogance and desire to control. In our attempt to play God, we created things unholy and unnatural in an attempt to control things that cannot be controlled. The limitless search for power and control, even as far as to separate ourselves from nature, of which we belong, and yet refuse to associate, is what will inevitably be our own downfall. Just my take from reading the novel, but goes to show how far the franchise has fallen. Its all about action now and scary dinosaurs, when the fear should really be emphasised on our desire to control that which is uncontrollable and will lead to our own extinction.

I honestly couldn’t care less about This Jurassic World: Rebirth or any more of the Jurassic World sequels at this point. Frankly In My Opinion It's just another cash-grab sequel in a franchise that used to had so much potential and creativity but has since lost its touch. The first Jurassic Park was groundbreaking and memorable, but now it feels like they’re just recycling the same formula with nostalgia bait by bringing back the old cast and dinosaurs to lure in fans. But what do we get in the end? Another lazy sequel, designed to make money rather than offer anything new or exciting. And don’t get me started on Camp Cretaceous or Chaos Theory they’re just as uninspired. I miss when Jurassic Park actually felt fresh and innovative. Universal Studios, or whoever is running this stupid franchise now, please just let it die already it’s time to stop.

After watching the animated show this franchise produced (`Jurassic World: Camp Cretaceous`), I think the Jurassic World franchise may just have some of the best writers in the industry working on their current projects.  The show utilizes deep rooted psychological fear triggers throughout the series to elicit fear responses, and was able to implement an interwoven storyline that weaved its own story into the events that take place in the Jurassic World main-line movies, in a way that truly made it feel like additional movie content & background lore, in a way that I have never seen in my lifetime. So I have high hopes for this new era, especially if they engage the team that helped develop and produce the Camp Cretaceous series in the story and screen writing process.

Die sollen mehr reale Dinosaurier raus bringen, die es wirklich gegeben hat statt Experimente und irgendwelche Hybriden. Es gibt noch so viele große Dinosaurier die in ihren Filmen  bisher nicht vorkamen. Elasmosaurus, Carcharodontosaurus, Leviathan, Lorrainosaurus, Liopleurodon, Tyrannotitan, Arizonasaurus und viele mehr und  noch größere Langhals Dinosaurier. Es wird langsam langweilig und kitschig. Es gibt so viele große unbekannte Dinosaurier Arten die nicht verfilmt wurden. Sehr schade finde ich das. Genauso könnte man ein komplett neues Kapitel anfangen, interstellar, auf einen anderen Planeten wo man ebenfalls Leben in  Form von Dinosaurier entdeckt hat, eine Erkundungsreise. Aus wissenschaftlicher Sicht ist das nicht ausgeschlossen dass solche Lebensformen wie hier auf der Erde, auch auf anderen Planeten existierten und oder sogar immer noch eventuell existierten. Man müsste nur mal in der Lage sein die Flugbahnen der damaligen Kometen, Asteroiden zurück  vollziehen zu können. Denn diese beinhalteten  die Grundsteine des Lebens. Dadurch ist damals alles Leben und Wasser auf der Erde und auf den anderen Planeten des Sonnensystems gelandet. Und so wird sich das auch in anderen Bereichen unserer Galaxie und anderer Galaxien abgespielt haben. Leider hört man nur sehr selten Leute darüber sprechen. Aber genau das wäre die perfekte Grundlage für ein komplett neues Kapitel von Jurassic World, Jurassic Galaxy, nur als Beispiel. Und wie bereits erwähnt, gibt es noch so viele große und kleine Dinosaurierarten die bisher nicht verfilmt wurden, was ich sehr bedauerlich finde. Und diese waren nicht weniger spektakulär wie T-Rex, Spinosaurus etc.

I think that this will take place on one of the five deaths, not Sorna or Nublar, here’s why, transporting dinosaurs from a place like Barbados to Sorna seems unlikely, and there are ruins of ancient civilizations. Okay but I’m gonna be honest I think the accurate spinos isn’t in the spirit or lore of Jurassic park. They are 100% going back to Isla Sorna, with them saying it was the original research grounds, and nobody, probably even biosyn, has touched sorna for over 20 years, and there’s no way Spinosaurs would be able to swim from the mainland to sorna. Plus, the original reason for the dinosaurs not looking accurate lore-wise was to fill in the gaps in the genomes with modern day animals. No animals look anywhere close to spinosaurus. Additionally, what spinosaurus looked like is still not concrete, so it could change in the coming years, like it has in the past, rendering this version inaccurate. Plus, the inaccurate spino is loved by so many people, despite the obvious inaccuracy.
